CFA Level 1 Curriculum

1041 lessons

Quantitative Methods

Module 0: Getting Started

  • 0.01 | CFA Program Overview and Information
  • 0.02 | Course Policies
  • 0.03 | Taking Your Exam
  • 0.04 | Getting Help
  • 0.05 | Errata and Feedback
  • 0.06 | Traits of Successful Candidates
  • 0.07 | Quantitative Methods Overview
  • 0.08 | Quantitative Methods Glossary

Module 1: Rates and Returns

  • Learning Outcomes: Rates and Returns
  • 1.01 | Introduction
  • 1.02 | Interest Rates and Time Value of Money
  • 1.03 | Rates of Return
  • 1.04 | Money-Weighted and Time-Weighted Return
  • 1.05 | Annualized Return
  • 1.06 | Other Major Return Measures and Their Applications
  • Glossary: Rates and Returns
  • Flashcards: Rates and Returns
  • Real-World Applications: Bloomberg's Cashflow Analyzer

Module 2: Time Value of Money in Finance

  • Learning Outcomes: Time Value of Money in Finance
  • 2.01 | Introduction
  • 2.02 | Time Value of Money in Fixed Income and Equity
  • 2.03 | Implied Return and Growth
  • 2.04 | Cash Flow Additivity
  • Glossary: Time Value of Money in Finance
  • Flashcards: Time Value of Money in Finance
  • Real-World Applications: Bloomberg's Dividend Discount Model

Module 3: Statistical Measures of Asset Returns

  • Learning Outcomes: Statistical Measures of Asset Returns
  • 3.01 | Introduction
  • 3.02 | Measures of Central Tendency and Location
  • 3.03 | Measures of Dispersion
  • 3.04 | Measures of Shape of a Distribution
  • 3.05 | Correlation between Two Variables
  • Glossary: Statistical Measures of Asset Returns
  • Flashcards: Statistical Measures of Asset Returns
  • Real-World Applications: Bloomberg's Historical Return Histogram

Module 4: Probability Trees and Conditional Expectations

  • Learning Outcomes: Probability Trees and Conditional Expectations
  • 4.01 | Introduction
  • 4.02 | Expected Value and Variance
  • 4.03 | Probability Trees and Conditional Expectations
  • 4.04 | Bayes' Formula and Updating Probability Estimates
  • Glossary: Probability Trees and Conditional Expectations
  • Flashcards: Probability Trees and Conditional Expectations
  • Real-World Applications: Bloomberg's Graph Price

Module 5: Portfolio Mathematics

  • Learning Outcomes: Portfolio Mathematics
  • 5.01 | Introduction
  • 5.02 | Portfolio Expected Return and Variance of Return
  • 5.03 | Forecasting Correlation of Returns: Covariance Given a Joint Probability Function
  • 5.04 | Portfolio Risk Measures: Applications of the Normal Distribution
  • Glossary: Portfolio Mathematics
  • Flashcards: Portfolio Mathematics
  • Real-World Applications: Bloomberg's Peer Correlation

Module 6: Simulation Methods

  • Learning Outcomes: Simulation Methods
  • 6.01 | Introduction
  • 6.02 | Lognormal Distribution and Continuous Compounding
  • 6.03 | Monte Carlo Simulation
  • 6.04 | Bootstrapping
  • Glossary: Simulation Methods
  • Flashcards: Simulation Methods
  • Real-World Applications: Bloomberg's Portfolio \u0026 Risk Analytics - Value At Risk

Module 7: Estimation and Inference

  • Learning Outcomes: Estimation and Inference
  • 7.01 | Introduction
  • 7.02 | Sampling Methods
  • 7.03 | Central Limit Theorem and Inference
  • 7.04 | Bootstrapping and Empirical Sampling Distributions
  • Glossary: Estimation and Inference
  • Flashcards: Estimation and Inference
  • Real-World Applications: Bloomberg's FX Rate Forecast Model

Module 8: Hypothesis Testing

  • Learning Outcomes: Hypothesis Testing
  • 8.01 | Introduction
  • 8.02 | Hypothesis Tests for Finance
  • 8.03 | Tests of Return and Risk in Finance
  • 8.04 | Parametric versus Nonparametric Tests
  • Glossary: Hypothesis Testing
  • Flashcards: Hypothesis Testing
  • Real-World Applications: Bloomberg's News On Drug Approvals

Module 9: Parametric and Non-Parametric Tests of Independence

  • Learning Outcomes: Parametric and Non-Parametric Tests of Independence
  • 9.01 | Introduction
  • 9.02 | Tests Concerning Correlation
  • 9.03 | Tests of Independence Using Contingency Table Data

General

  • Glossary: Parametric and Non-Parametric Tests of Independence
  • Flashcards: Parametric and Non-Parametric Tests of Independence
  • Real-World Applications: Bloomberg's Regression Analysis
  • Learning Outcomes: Simple Linear Regression
  • Flashcards: Introduction to Big Data Techniques
  • Real-World Applications: Bloomberg's News Feed Artificial Intelligence
  • 12.01 | Appendix A: Cumulative Probabilities for a Standard Normal Distribution
  • 12.02 | Appendix B: Table of the Student’s t-Distribution (One-Tailed Probabilities)
  • 12.03 | Appendix C: Values of χ2 (Degrees of Freedom, Level of Significance)
  • 12.04 | Appendix D: Table of the F-Distribution
  • 12.05 | Appendix E: Critical Values for the Durbin-Watson Statistic (α = .05)

Module 10: Simple Linear Regression

  • 10.01 | Introduction
  • 10.02 | Estimation of the Simple Linear Regression Model
  • 10.03 | Assumptions of the Simple Linear Regression Model
  • 10.04 | Hypothesis Tests in the Simple Linear Regression Model
  • 10.05 | Prediction in the Simple Linear Regression Model
  • 10.06 | Functional Forms for Simple Linear Regression
  • Glossary: Simple Linear Regression
  • Flashcards: Simple Linear Regression
  • Real-World Applications: Bloomberg's Historical Beta

Module 11: Introduction to Big Data Techniques

  • Learning Outcomes: Introduction to Big Data Techniques
  • 11.01 | Introduction
  • 11.02 | How Is Fintech used in Quantitative Investment Analysis?
  • 11.03 | Advanced Analytical Tools: Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
  • 11.04 | Tackling Big Data with Data Science
  • Glossary: Introduction to Big Data Techniques

Module 1: The Firm and Market Structures

  • Learning Outcomes: The Firm and Market Structures
  • 1.01 | Introduction
  • 1.02 | Profit Maximization: Production Breakeven, Shutdown and Economies of Scale
  • 1.03 | Introduction to Market Structures
  • 1.04 | Monopolistic Competition
  • 1.05 | Oligopoly
  • 1.06 | Determining Market Structure
  • Glossary: The Firm and Market Structures
  • Flashcards: The Firm and Market Structures

  • Learning Outcomes: Understanding Business Cycles
  • 2.01 | Introduction
  • 2.02 | Overview of the Business Cycle
  • 2.03 | Credit Cycles
  • 2.04 | Economic Indicators over the Business Cycle
  • Glossary: Understanding Business Cycles

  • Learning Outcomes: Fiscal Policy
  • 3.01 | Introduction
  • 3.02 | Introduction to Monetary and Fiscal Policy
  • 3.03 | Roles and Objectives of Fiscal Policy
  • 3.04 | Fiscal Policy Tools
  • 3.05 | Fiscal Policy Implementation

  • Learning Outcomes: Monetary Policy
  • 4.01 | Introduction
  • 4.02 | Role of Central Banks
  • 4.03 | Monetary Policy Tools and Monetary Transmission
  • 4.04 | Monetary Policy Objectives
  • 4.05 | Interaction of Monetary and Fiscal Policy

  • Real-World Applications: Bloomberg's Overnight Lending Rates

Module 5: Introduction to Geopolitics

  • Learning Outcomes: Introduction to Geopolitics
  • 5.01 | Introduction
  • 5.02 | National Governments and Political Cooperation
  • 5.03 | Forces of Globalization
  • 5.04 | International Trade Organizations
  • 5.05 | Assessing Geopolitical Actors and Risk
  • 5.06 | The Tools of Geopolitics
  • 5.07 | Geopolitical Risk and the Investment Process
  • Glossary: Introduction to Geopolitics
  • Flashcards: Introduction to Geopolitics
  • Real-World Applications: Bloomberg's News Feed On Macro

Module 6: International Trade

  • Learning Outcomes: International Trade
  • 6.01 | Introduction
  • 6.02 | Benefits and Costs of Trade
  • 6.03 | Trade Restrictions and Agreements—Tariffs, Quotas, and Export Subsidies
  • 6.04 | Trading Blocs and Regional Integration

  • Flashcards: International Trade
  • Real-World Applications: Bloomberg's Trade Flow

Module 7: Capital Flows and the Fx Market

  • Learning Outcomes: Capital Flows and the FX Market
  • 7.01 | Introduction
  • 7.02 | The Foreign Exchange Market and Exchange Rates
  • 7.03 | Exchange Rate Regimes: Ideals and Historical Perspective
  • 7.04 | Capital Restrictions

  • Flashcards: Capital Flows and the FX Market
  • Real-World Applications: Bloomberg's Foreign Portfolio Investment

Module 8: Exchange Rate Calculations

  • Learning Outcomes: Exchange Rate Calculations
  • 8.01 | Introduction
  • 8.02 | Cross-Rate Calculations
  • 8.03 | Forward Rate Calculations

  • 10.01 | Introduction

  • 10.03 | GAAP, Decision Useful Financial Reporting
  • 10.04 | Biased Accounting Choices
  • 10.05 | Departures from GAAP

  • 10.11 | Accounting Choices That Affect the Cash Flow Statement

  • 10.13 | Warning Signs

  • 11.03 | Analytical Tools and Techniques
  • 11.04 | Financial Ratio Analysis

  • 11.10 | Liquidity Ratios

  • Glossary: Fixed-Income Markets for Government Issuers
